title: Controllability of the cubic Schroedinger equation via a low-dimensional   source term
comment:
28 pages; added references; substantial change of main results,
  formulated now for NLS on a torus of arbitrary dimension
summary:
We study controllability of $d$-dimensional defocusing cubic Schroedinger
equation under periodic boundary conditions. The control is applied additively,
via a source term, which is a linear combination of few complex exponentials
(modes) with time-variant coefficients - controls. We manage to prove that
controlling at most $2^d$ modes one can achieve controllability of the equation
in any finite-dimensional projection of the evolution space
$H^{s}(\mathbb{T}^d), \ s>d/2$, as well as approximate controllability in
$H^{s}(\mathbb{T}^d)$. We also present negative result regarding exact
controllability of cubic Schroedinger equation via a finite-dimensional source
term.

title: On the validity of formal asymptotic expansions in Allen-Cahn equation   and FitzHugh-Nagumo system with generic initial data
summary:
Formal asymptotic expansions have long been used to study the singularly
perturbed Allen-Cahn type equations and reaction-diffusion systems, including
in particular the FitzHugh-Nagumo system. Despite their successful role, it has
been largely unclear whether or not such expansions really represent the actual
profile of solutions with rather general initial data. By combining our earlier
result and known properties of eternal solutions of the Allen-Cahn equation, we
prove validity of the principal term of the formal expansions for a large class
of solutions.
